description: Tooth-tip ridge location + continuity gate. Two U-shaped merges side by side: LEFT has a 3-column-wide bottom with a covered-cell tooth (handled by the pre-existing covered-cell path); RIGHT has a 2-column-wide bottom with a rail tooth whose tip sits a full rail-width past the flanking cell edge. The rail tooth is what exposed the bug where tooth-tip fillets + OA cuts landed mid-tooth instead of at the capping bridge. Both teeth must round their tips on the underlying surface with continuous (gap-free) side ridges. Wide rails (gph 77.9 vs cell 30) make any mislocation obvious. The fix also buries the ridge-end-chamfer divots (via _mridge overlap) and retracts the side/cap transverses to the tip aridge tangent (the general _tt_dir != "" rule), so both teeth show no tabs, divots, or breaks.

steps.label: both U-merge teeth round their tips with continuous side ridges (covered-cell tooth vs rail tooth)
